title: गायत्री सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री गायत्री सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम् ॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
मुक्ताविद्रुमहेमनीलधवलच्छायैर्मुखैस्त्रीक्षणैः
युक्तामिन्दुनिबद्धरत्नमकुटां तत्त्वार्थवर्णात्मिकाम्।
गायत्रीं वरदाभयाङ्कुशकशाश्शुभ्रंकपालं गुणं
शंखं चक्रमथारविन्दयुगलं हस्तैर्वहन्तीं भजे॥

॥ स्तोत्रम् ॥
ॐ गायत्री सावित्री च सरस्वती च सन्ध्या त्रिसन्ध्या मुनिमातृका।
वेदमाता वेदगर्भा वेदनेत्री विधात्रिणी॥ १॥

त्रिपदा त्रिगुणा तेजस्त्रिमूर्तिस्त्रिदिवेश्वरी।
त्रैलोक्यवन्दिता त्रैतोत्तमा त्रिवर्णा त्रिलोचना॥ २॥

चतुर्वेदमयी चण्डा चतुर्मुखसमुद्भवा।
चराचरमयी चित्रा चन्द्रकोटिसमप्रभा॥ ३॥

पञ्चमुखी पञ्चवक्त्रा पञ्चभूतात्मिका परा।
पद्मासना पद्महस्ता पद्मपत्रनिभेक्षणा॥ ४॥

ब्रह्माणी ब्रह्मदात्री च ब्रह्मवादिनी ब्रह्मजा।
ब्रह्मतेजोमयी बृन्दा ब्रह्मविद्याप्रदायिनी॥ ५॥

सावित्री सत्यसन्धा च सर्वदेवनमस्कृता।
सर्वशास्त्रमयी सिद्धा सर्वपापविमोचनी॥ ६॥

महामाया महामेधा महाश्री महासुखा।
महाविद्या महावाणी महाशक्तिर्महाद्युतिः॥ ७॥

दशमुखी दशभुजा दशदिक्पालसेविता।
दयापूर्णा दुरित्हन्त्री दरिद्र्यविनाशिनी॥ ८॥

सूर्यमण्डलसंस्थाना सूर्यकोटिसमप्रभा।
सूर्यतेजोमयी सन्ध्या सूर्यमाता सदागतिः॥ ९॥

विश्वामित्रप्रसूता च वेदवन्द्या विभावरी।
विद्या विद्यावती विद्या विमला विश्वधारिणी॥ १०॥

आदिशक्तिरनाद्यन्ता अक्षरा अमलात्मिका।
अग्निमुखी अमृतास्वादा अमृतानन्दरूपिणी॥ ११॥

जपमाता जपसिद्धा जपफलप्रदायिनी।
जगत्सृष्टिकरी जन्मजरामृत्युविनाशिनी॥ १२॥

तत्सवितुर्वरेण्यस्य भर्गो देवस्य धीमहि।
धियो यो नः प्रचोदयादिति गायत्रीमन्त्ररूपिणी॥ १३॥

प्रातःसन्ध्या ब्रह्मरूपा मध्याह्ने वैष्णवी स्मृता।
सायंसन्ध्या शिवरूपा त्रिसन्ध्यात्मा सनातनी॥ १४॥

कुण्डलिनी कामधात्री कल्याणी कमलालया।
कामदा कामरूपा च कृपापूर्णा कृतागमा॥ १५॥

॥ फलश्रुति ॥
गायत्रीसहस्रनामस्तोत्रमिदं पुण्यं यः पठेत् प्रयतः शुचिः।
सर्वपापविनिर्मुक्तो ब्रह्मलोकं स गच्छति॥

त्रिसन्ध्यं पठनाद् विद्यां मेधां प्रज्ञां च विन्दति।
सर्वतीर्थफलं चैव गायत्रीजपतो लभेत्॥

ब्राह्मणः क्षत्रियो वैश्यः शूद्रो वा यः पठेत् सदा।
सर्ववर्णेषु पूज्यः स्यात् गायत्रीप्रसादतः सदा॥

॥ इति श्री गायत्री सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Gayatri Sahasranama Stotram ||

|| Dhyanam ||
Mukta-vidruma-hema-nila-dhavala-chchhayair-mukhais-trikshanaih
Yuktam-indu-nibaddha-ratna-makutam Tattvartha-varna-atmikam.
Gayatrim Varada-abhaya-ankusha-kashas-shubhram-kapalam Gunam
Shankham Chakram-atha-aravinda-yugalam Hastair-vahantim Bhaje.

|| Stotram ||
Om Gayatri Savitri Cha Saraswati Cha Sandhya Tri-sandhya Muni-matrika.
Veda-mata Veda-garbha Veda-netri Vidhatrini. || 1 ||

Tri-pada Tri-guna Tejas-tri-murtis-tri-diveshwari.
Trailokya-vandita Traita-uttama Tri-varna Tri-lochana. || 2 ||

Chatur-veda-mayi Chanda Chatur-mukha-samudbhava.
Chara-achara-mayi Chitra Chandra-koti-sama-prabha. || 3 ||

Pancha-mukhi Pancha-vaktra Pancha-bhuta-atmika Para.
Padma-asana Padma-hasta Padma-patra-nibhekshana. || 4 ||

Brahmaani Brahma-datri Cha Brahma-vadini Brahma-ja.
Brahma-tejo-mayi Brinda Brahma-vidya-pradayini. || 5 ||

Savitri Satya-sandha Cha Sarva-deva-namaskrita.
Sarva-shastra-mayi Siddha Sarva-papa-vimochani. || 6 ||

Mahamaya Mahamedha Mahashri Mahasukha.
Mahavidya Mahavani Mahashaktir-mahadyutih. || 7 ||

Dasha-mukhi Dasha-bhuja Dasha-dik-pala-sevita.
Daya-purna Durit-hantri Daridrya-vinashini. || 8 ||

Surya-mandala-samsthana Surya-koti-sama-prabha.
Surya-tejo-mayi Sandhya Surya-mata Sada-gatih. || 9 ||

Vishvamitra-prasuta Cha Veda-vandya Vibhavari.
Vidya Vidyavati Vidya Vimala Vishva-dharini. || 10 ||

Adi-shaktir-anadi-anta Akshara Amala-atmika.
Agni-mukhi Amrita-asvada Amrita-ananda-rupini. || 11 ||

Japa-mata Japa-siddha Japa-phala-pradayini.
Jagat-srishti-kari Janma-jara-mrityu-vinashini. || 12 ||

Tat-savitur-varenyasya Bhargo Devasya Dhimahi.
Dhiyo Yo Nah Prachodayad-iti Gayatri-mantra-rupini. || 13 ||

Pratah-sandhya Brahma-rupa Madhyahne Vaishnavi Smrita.
Sayam-sandhya Shiva-rupa Tri-sandhya-atma Sanatani. || 14 ||

Kundalini Kama-dhatri Kalyani Kamala-alaya.
Kamada Kama-rupa Cha Kripa-purna Krita-agama. || 15 ||

|| Phalashruti ||
Gayatri-sahasra-nama-stotram-idam Punyam Yah Pathet Prayatah Shuchih.
Sarva-papa-vinirmukto Brahma-lokam Sa Gachchhati.

Tri-sandhyam Pathanad Vidyam Medham Prajnam Cha Vindati.
Sarva-tirtha-phalam Chaiva Gayatri-japato Labhet.

Brahmanah Kshatriyo Vaishyah Shudro Va Yah Pathet Sada.
Sarva-varneshu Pujyah Syat Gayatri-prasadatah Sada.

|| Iti Shri Gayatri Sahasranama Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
मुक्ता, विद्रुम, स्वर्ण, नील, श्वेत छायाओं वाले मुखों, तीन नेत्रों, चन्द्रमा जड़ित रत्नमुकुट, तत्त्वार्थवर्णात्मिका, वरद-अभय-अंकुश-कशा-कपाल-गुण-शंख-चक्र-कमलयुग्म हाथों में धारण करने वाली गायत्री का भजन करता हूँ।

गायत्री, सावित्री, सरस्वती, सन्ध्या, त्रिसन्ध्या, वेदमाता, वेदगर्भा, त्रिपदा, त्रिगुणा, चतुर्वेदमयी, पञ्चमुखी, पद्मासना, ब्रह्माणी, ब्रह्मविद्याप्रदायिनी, महामाया, महामेधा, सूर्यमण्डलस्थित, सूर्यकोटिप्रभा, आदिशक्ति, जपमाता, गायत्रीमन्त्ररूपिणी, प्रातःसन्ध्या ब्रह्मरूप, मध्याह्न वैष्णवी, सायंसन्ध्या शिवरूप, कुण्डलिनी - इन नामों से गायत्री देवी की स्तुति की गई है।

जो पवित्र होकर इस सहस्रनाम का पाठ करता है, वह सर्वपापमुक्त होकर ब्रह्मलोक को जाता है। त्रिसन्ध्या पाठ से विद्या, मेधा, प्रज्ञा प्राप्त होती है और सर्वतीर्थ का फल मिलता है।

viniyog.kilak: श्री गायत्री देवी प्रसाद सिद्ध्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः
viniyog.shloka: ॐ अस्य श्री गायत्री सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रस्य विश्वामित्र ऋषिः, अनुष्टुप् छन्दः, श्री गायत्री देवता, ॐ भूर्भुवः स्वः बीजम्, तत्सवितुर्वरेण्यम् शक्तिः, श्री गायत्री देवी प्रसाद सिद्ध्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः।

benefits: Invokes the thousand names of Gayatri Devi, the mother of Vedas
Invokes the blessing of the merit of visiting all holy pilgrimages
Purifies all sins and grants Brahmaloka
Especially powerful during Sandhya times and Vasant Panchami
Awakens Kundalini shakti and promotes spiritual evolution
Invokes success in education, examinations, and intellectual pursuits
description: The Gayatri Sahasranama Stotram, known in Sanskrit as गायत्री सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम्, is a sacred recitation of 1000 divine names in praise of Goddess Gayatri. Rooted in Devi Bhagavata Purana, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to grant success in education, examinations, and intellectual pursuits as well as bestow the merit of visiting all holy pilgrimages. Additionally, it is known to purify all sins and grant Brahmaloka. It is especially recommended on Sunday (Ravivar) and Thursday (Guruvar) and during Vasant Panchami and Guru Purnima for maximum spiritual benefit. At 15 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 30 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Gayatri Sahasranama Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Gayatri Sahasranama in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. 1000 names of Gayatri Devi for wisdom, knowledge, and spiritual liberation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Gayatri Sahasranama and where is it preserved?
seoFaqs.answer: The Gayatri Sahasranama Stotram is a Sanskrit hymn enumerating one thousand names of Devi Gayatri, the personified mantra-shakti of the Gayatri mantra (Rigveda 3.62.10) and the Vedamata (mother of the Vedas). The most authoritative version is preserved in the Devi Bhagavata Purana (twelfth skandha), and a parallel version exists in the Brahma Vaivarta Purana and the Shakta Maha Bhagavata. The opening dhyana describes her as panchamukhi (five-faced) — the faces being mukta (pearl-white), vidruma (coral-red), hema (golden), nila (sapphire-blue) and dhavala (snow-white) — three-eyed in each face, ten-armed, crowned with the moon, holding shankha, chakra, gada, padma, ankusha, pasha and varada-abhaya mudras. (Sources: Devi Bhagavata Purana 12; Brahma Vaivarta Purana; Stotra Nidhi Gayatri.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Devi Gayatri and what is her relationship to the Gayatri mantra?
seoFaqs.answer: Devi Gayatri is the personification of the Gayatri mantra — Om Bhur Bhuvah Svah Tat Savitur Varenyam Bhargo Devasya Dheemahi Dhiyo Yo Nah Prachodayat — the most sacred Vedic mantra revealed to Vishvamitra and preserved in the third mandala of the Rigveda. As Vedamata, she is the mother of all four Vedas, the consort of Brahma in his role as creator, and the source of all sacred knowledge (vidya). Her three names — Gayatri (in the Brahma form), Savitri (in the Vishnu form), and Saraswati (in the Rudra form) — correspond to the three sandhya periods (morning, noon, evening) when sandhyavandanam is performed. She is the principal devata of the upanayana (sacred-thread) initiation. (Sources: Rigveda 3.62.10; Brihadaranyaka Upanishad; Manu Smriti; Hindupedia Gayatri.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the iconography of Panchamukhi Gayatri described in the Sahasranama?
seoFaqs.answer: The Sahasranama dhyana describes Gayatri as panchamukhi — the five faces representing the five elements (pancha-bhuta) and the five sheaths (pancha-kosha): mukta-mukha (pearl-white face for ether/anandamaya kosha), vidruma-mukha (coral-red for fire/manomaya), hema-mukha (golden for earth/annamaya), nila-mukha (blue for air/pranamaya), dhavala-mukha (white for water/vijnanamaya). Each face has three eyes, and she has ten arms holding the eight major attributes (shankha, chakra, gada, padma, ankusha, pasha, kapala, kasha) plus varada and abhaya mudras. She is crowned with the moon, seated on a red lotus over the hamsa (swan), her vahana shared with Brahma. This panchamukhi iconography is uniquely Gayatri's. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi Gayatri dhyana; Devi Bhagavata; Hindupedia.)

seoFaqs.question: When should Gayatri Sahasranama be chanted for best results?
seoFaqs.answer: The principal occasion is Gayatri Jayanti, the eleventh tithi of Shukla paksha Jyeshtha (May-June, immediately after Ganga Dussehra), when the Sahasranama is recited at every Gayatri Pariwar centre, Shantikunj Haridwar (the international headquarters of Gayatri Pariwar founded by Pandit Shriram Sharma Acharya), and most Vedic ashramas. It is also chanted during the three sandhyas (sunrise, noon, sunset) by upanayana-initiated brahmins, on every Friday (Devi day), during all nine nights of Navaratri, and on Vasant Panchami when Gayatri is invoked as Saraswati. Daily morning recitation is recommended for those seeking buddhi (intellect), vidya (learning), and spiritual liberation. (Sources: Drikpanchang Gayatri Jayanti; Shantikunj Haridwar guides; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Gayatri Sahasranama promise according to phalashruti?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti declares the Sahasranama grants buddhi-vridhi (increase of intellect), vidya (knowledge of all sciences and Vedas), tejas (spiritual radiance), arogya (health), ayushyam (longevity), removal of all paapas including brahma-hatya, freedom from all eighty-four lakhs of yonis (rebirths), purification of all karmas, attainment of brahmaloka after death, and ultimately moksha through identification with the Vedamata-tattva. Tradition holds that one who recites the Gayatri Sahasranama daily becomes a jivanmukta (liberated while living) within forty mandalas. The Devi Bhagavata Purana states that even hearing the Sahasranama once destroys the sins of seven births. (Sources: Devi Bhagavata Purana 12 phalashruti; Stotra Nidhi; Hindupedia.)

seoFaqs.question: How is Gayatri Sahasranama related to the Sandhyavandanam ritual?
seoFaqs.answer: The Sandhyavandanam is the thrice-daily Vedic ritual performed by all upanayana-initiated brahmin males (and increasingly by women in modern revivals), centred on the recitation of the Gayatri mantra 108 times at each sandhya. The Gayatri Sahasranama is recited as an extended adoration of the Devi after the basic mantra-japa, especially during the morning sandhya before sunrise and on Sundays, Fridays, and special tithis. Many Smarta brahmins recite the Sahasranama on the day after upakarma (Shravana Purnima Yajurvedi/Avani Avittam), on Gayatri Jayanti, and as part of pitru-tarpana ceremonies. The Sahasranama is the highest praise of the very Devi being invoked in the basic mantra. (Sources: Apastamba Sandhyavandana sutras; Smarta parampara guides; Hindupedia.)

seoFaqs.question: How is Gayatri Sahasranama related to Lalita Sahasranama and Saraswati Sahasranama?
seoFaqs.answer: All three are major Devi sahasranamas reflecting different aspects of the same Adi Shakti. Gayatri Sahasranama (Devi Bhagavata) emphasises Vedamata, the mantra-shakti, and the patron of vidya and tapas. Lalita Sahasranama (Brahmanda Purana) emphasises Tripura Sundari, the supreme cosmic beauty and Sri Vidya devata. Saraswati Sahasranama (Skanda Purana) emphasises the goddess of speech, music, learning and creative arts. The Gayatri Pariwar tradition treats Gayatri as the supreme synthesis encompassing all three, while the Sri Vidya tradition treats Lalita as supreme and Gayatri as her vidya-shakti aspect. Many devotees recite Gayatri Sahasranama at sunrise, Saraswati Sahasranama on Vasant Panchami and Fridays, and Lalita Sahasranama on Fridays and Pournami. (Sources: Devi Bhagavata; Brahmanda Purana; Skanda Purana; Hindupedia.)
